message.content:
## Source Extractor

Research question: "Competitive landscape and market analysis for AI-powered recruitment platforms targeting solo/independent recruiters and small recruiting agencies (teams of 5-10), with a freemium model (free individual tier + paid team tier).

Research these dimensions specifically:
1. Existing recruitment platforms (ATS, recruiting CRM, applicant tracking) that target solo recruiters and small agencies — e.g. Recruit CRM, Loxo, Crelate, Manatal, JobAdder, Zoho Recruit, Bullhorn, RecruiterFlow, Vincere, iSmartRecruit, Ceipal, Tracker, Hireflow, hiring tools like Workable, Ashby, Greenhouse (note which are NOT for small/solo). For each relevant one: target customer, pricing/tiers, free tier availability, AI features, key strengths/weaknesses.
2. Which of these use a freemium or free-tier model (Zoho Recruit free plan, etc.) and how they monetize. How "easy to start" / self-serve onboarding is positioned.
3. Where AI is being applied in recruiting today (resume parsing, candidate matching/ranking, sourcing, outreach email generation, interview scheduling, candidate Q&A, job description writing, chrome extensions for sourcing from LinkedIn). Which features are table-stakes vs differentiators in 2025-2026.
4. The pain points of small/independent recruiters: data scattered across WhatsApp/Excel/Google Drive, time spent on admin, lack of context on past placements, no revenue visibility. What tools claim to solve these and gaps that remain.
5. Pricing benchmarks: typical per-seat/month pricing for recruiting CRM/ATS at the small-agency level, and what free tiers include.
6. Market size and trends for recruitment software / recruiting CRM, especially the SMB / independent recruiter segment."

Fetch and extract key claims from this source:
**URL:** https://www.selectsoftwarereviews.com/buyer-guide/free-recruiting-software
**Title:** 11 Best Free Recruitment Software of 2026, Tested & Reviewed - SSR
**Found via:** freemium/monetization & self-serve search

## Task
1. Use WebFetch to retrieve the page content.
2. Assess source quality: primary research/institution? secondary reporting? blog/opinion? forum? unreliable?
3. Extract 2-5 FALSIFIABLE claims that bear on the research question. Each claim must:
   - be a concrete, checkable statement (not vague generalities)
   - include a direct quote from the source as support
   - be rated central/supporting/tangential to the research question
4. Note publish date if available.

If the fetch fails or the page is irrelevant/paywalled, return claims: [] and sourceQuality: "unreliable".

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll fetch the page content and extract relevant claims.

input.prompt: Extract claims about free recruiting software for solo recruiters and small agencies. Specifically: which platforms offer free tiers, what those free tiers include, pricing for paid tiers, AI features, target customers, and any market/pricing benchmarks. List the specific tools reviewed (e.g. Zoho Recruit, Recruit CRM, Manatal, etc.), their free plan details and paid pricing. Include the publish date and any author/methodology info. Quote exact text where possible.

content.content:
# Free Recruiting Software: Key Findings

**Publication Details:**
- Last Updated: May 20, 2026
- Written by Anh Nguyen (Ex-Tech Recruiter, HR Tech Researcher)
- Contributing Expert: Phil Strazzulla

---

## Platforms Offering Free Tiers

### 1. **Manatal**
- **Free Offering:** 14-day trial (no credit card required)
- **Paid Tier:** Starts at $15/month per user
- **AI Features:** "AI-powered candidate filtering and recommendations"
- **Target:** Teams testing before purchase; SMBs sourcing English applicants
- **Limitation:** No free-forever plan available

### 2. **Recruit CRM**
- **Free Offering:** "Timeless free trial with no credit card information required"
- **Paid Tier:** Starts at $85/month per user
- **Features:** Core ATS, CRM, LinkedIn integration, resume parser (49 resumes)
- **Target:** Small employment agencies
- **Limitation:** Limited to one sequence and hiring pipeline on free tier

### 3. **Gem**
- **Free Offering:** 6 months free for startups under 30 employees; 50% off first year for teams under 100
- **Paid Tier:** Discounted rates after promotional period
- **AI Features:** "Unlimited AI agents for sourcing, application review"
- **Target:** Early-stage startups (excludes staffing/VC/PE firms)
- **Includes:** ATS, CRM, sourcing, scheduling, 800M+ profile database

### 4. **Zoho Recruit**
- **Free Offering:** Free-forever plan for one active job
- **Paid Tier:** Starts at $25/recruiter per month
- **Storage:** 256MB limit on free tier
- **Features:** Email management, interview scheduling, weekday support
- **Target:** In-house recruitment teams and agencies

### 5. **Breezy HR**
- **Free Offering:** "Forever" free plan (Bootstrap tier)
- **Paid Tiers:** $157–$439/month (billed annually)
- **Resume Parser:** Handles "20+ languages" with searchable results
- **Limitation:** Access to candidates added within 30 days only; 1 active job
- **Target:** Solo recruiters and small teams with minimal hiring needs

### 6. **Wellfound (formerly AngelList Talent)**
- **Free Offering:** Free-forever ATS with unlimited job postings
- **Paid Tier:** Starts at $149/month
- **Target:** Startups on budget; tech talent focus
- **Limitation:** No sourcing tools or AI features in free tier; lacks third-party job board integrations

### 7. **Jobin.cloud**
- **Free Offering:** Free-forever plan with permanent access
- **Features:** LinkedIn sourcing, CRM (10,000 contacts), 15 daily LinkedIn messages, 100 daily emails
- **AI:** "ChatGPT-powered personalization with 30 free AI prompts"
- **Target:** Solo recruiters, lean talent teams
- **Limitation:** Basic ATS (one pipeline only); dated interface

### 8. **GoHire**
- **Free Offering:** 14-day free trial (all paid features included)
- **Paid Tiers:** $99–$299/month
- **AI Features:** Job descriptions, career pages, interview questions, onboarding checklists
- **Target:** Early-stage startups and small businesses
- **Special:** Discounts for nonprofits and early-stage companies

### 9. **Recooty**
- **Free Offering:** 14-day trial (5 active jobs); free plan post-trial (1 active job)
- **Paid Tiers:** Start at $99/month
- **Features:** 250+ job boards, AI job description generator
- **Languages:** 8 supported languages
- **Target:** Startups and small businesses
- **Limitation:** Single-user free plan; no Chrome extension

### 10. **Indeed**
- **Free Offering:** Free job posting to general search results; $1,000 trial sponsorship
- **Paid:** Sponsored listings vary by location/title
- **Target:** Any business size
- **Limitation:** Free posts have low visibility; not a replacement for ATS

### 11. **Calendly**
- **Free Offering:** Forever-free plan for interview scheduling
- **Target:** Interview scheduling focused

---

## Key Market Benchmarks

- **Pricing Range:** Free trials to $25–$299/month (paid tiers)
- **Common Limitations:** Most free tiers restrict to 1 active job; limited storage (256MB on Zoho)
- **AI Inclusion:** Premium feature across platforms; some trials include AI tools
- **Target Audience:** Solo recruiters, agencies, startups under 100 employees

---

## Methodology

Per the guide: evaluation prioritized "range of functions, support resources, user interface, customization features, and long-term potential" using software demos, personal testing, and expert insights.

content.text: The fetch succeeded. SSR (SelectSoftwareReviews) is a hands-on software review site with named authors and a stated testing methodology. Extracting falsifiable claims relevant to the research question.

claims.claim: Zoho Recruit offers a free-forever plan limited to one active job with 256MB storage, and its paid tiers start at $25/recruiter per month.
claims.quote: Zoho Recruit ... Free Offering: Free-forever plan for one active job; Paid Tier: Starts at $25/recruiter per month; Storage: 256MB limit on free tier

claims.claim: Recruit CRM targets small employment agencies and starts at $85/month per user, offering a free trial (not a free-forever plan) limited to one sequence and one hiring pipeline.
claims.quote: Recruit CRM ... Paid Tier: Starts at $85/month per user ... Target: Small employment agencies ... Limitation: Limited to one sequence and hiring pipeline on free tier

claims.claim: Manatal targets SMBs and offers only a 14-day trial (no free-forever plan) with paid tiers starting at $15/month per user, including AI-powered candidate filtering and recommendations.
claims.quote: Manatal ... Free Offering: 14-day trial (no credit card required); Paid Tier: Starts at $15/month per user; AI Features: "AI-powered candidate filtering and recommendations" ... Limitation: No free-forever plan available

claims.claim: Jobin.cloud offers a free-forever plan aimed at solo recruiters that includes LinkedIn sourcing, a CRM with 10,000 contacts, 15 daily LinkedIn messages, 100 daily emails, and 30 free ChatGPT-powered AI prompts, but only a single ATS pipeline.
claims.quote: Jobin.cloud ... Free-forever plan with permanent access ... LinkedIn sourcing, CRM (10,000 contacts), 15 daily LinkedIn messages, 100 daily emails ... "ChatGPT-powered personalization with 30 free AI prompts" ... Target: Solo recruiters, lean talent teams ... Limitation: Basic ATS (one pipeline only)

claims.claim: Across free recruiting tools, AI features (e.g. job description generation, candidate filtering) are positioned as a premium/paid feature, and most free tiers restrict users to one active job.
claims.quote: AI Inclusion: Premium feature across platforms; some trials include AI tools ... Common Limitations: Most free tiers restrict to 1 active job

content.text: Extracted 5 falsifiable claims from the SSR free recruiting software guide. The source is a secondary software review site (SelectSoftwareReviews) with named authors (Anh Nguyen, ex-tech recruiter; Phil Strazzulla) and a stated hands-on testing methodology, last updated 2026-05-20. Claims cover free-tier availability, paid pricing benchmarks ($15–$85/user/mo), AI feature positioning, and target customers for solo recruiters and small agencies — all central to the research question.
